title: CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst
Identify key diagnostic features of CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst
Apply clinical reasoning to CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst management
Recognize common pitfalls in CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst

sections.title: Overview of CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst
sections.content:
**CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst**

Congenital pulmonary airway malformation types, bronchogenic cyst pathogenesis and location

**Key Points:**
• CPAM (formerly CCAM): hamartomatous overgrowth of terminal bronchioles
• Type 1 (most common, best prognosis): large cysts >2cm
• Type 2: small cysts <2cm, associated with other anomalies

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• CPAM (formerly CCAM): hamartomatous overgrowth of terminal bronchioles
• Type 1 (most common, best prognosis): large cysts >2cm

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Type 2: small cysts <2cm, associated with other anomalies
• Type 3 (worst prognosis): microcystic/solid, large lesions
• Bronchogenic cyst: abnormal budding of tracheobronchial tree, fluid-filled, mediastinal or intrapulmonary

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst:**

• Type 2: small cysts <2cm, associated with other anomalies
• Type 3 (worst prognosis): microcystic/solid, large lesions
• Bronchogenic cyst: abnormal budding of tracheobronchial tree, fluid-filled, mediastinal or intrapulmonary

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst?

options.text: CPAM (formerly CCAM): hamartomatous overgrowth of terminal bronchioles
options.explanation: CPAM (formerly CCAM): hamartomatous overgrowth of terminal bronchioles

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst?

options.text: Bronchogenic cyst: abnormal budding of tracheobronchial tree, fluid-filled, mediastinal or intrapulmonary
options.explanation: Bronchogenic cyst: abnormal budding of tracheobronchial tree, fluid-filled, mediastinal or intrapulmonary

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with CPAM & Bronchogenic Cyst. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Type 1 (most common, best prognosis): large cysts >2cm
options.explanation: Type 1 (most common, best prognosis): large cysts >2cm
